track: 3 — recall/discovery (a mixed record: ONE genuine, structurally-verified IF-8 fire recorded GATED + a clean Shape-B precision-silence across the other four families — on a new stack: Java, multi-module Maven)
shape: A+B — IF-8 FIRED (a real 33-package cross-import cycle, recorded GATED / not filed) + IF-6/IF-10/IF-7c/IF-3a stayed silent (0 genuine fires / 26 named suppressions total / 0 false defects). The headline is a LANGUAGE CONTRAST: Java/javac permits cross-package cycles, so the IF-8 fire is a REAL structural finding (the opposite of caddy's suppressed Go component-SCC artifact and ripgrep's structurally-impossible Rust DAG).
engine_proxy: deterministic-detector-sweep (5 read-only agents — IF-8 / IF-6 / IF-10 / IF-7c / IF-3a — applying the SHIPPED deterministic predicates (§21/§24+§28/§25+§29/§23/AF-1) directly to the real Java source; NOT a full Detect→Emit pipeline; no cost ledger recorded). Build chain:  (5 agents, ~281.6k tokens, 92 tool-uses, ~602s). The IF-8 strongly-connected-component result + the 38 minimal 2-node mutual-import pairs were INDEPENDENTLY RE-COMPUTED by the main loop (a separate Tarjan SCC over the grep'd package-import edge list) and agree exactly — two independent computations converged on the same 38 closing pairs and the same Core members. The IF-7c agent surfaced the 4 empty/comment-only catch sites but ran out of budget before finalizing; its adjudication (each suppressed, named below) was COMPLETED + verified line-by-line by the main loop.

repo.name: signalapp/Signal-Server
repo.pinned_sha_note: shallow-clone HEAD at 2026-06-12; the file:line citations are the durable anchors.
repo.license: AGPL-3.0 (copyleft — DISCLOSED; DeepInit's use is read-only/local = non-derivative; the moonshot/upstream bar is EXTRA-hard for this repo and any business-rule finding is framed as an access-control/business-rule contradiction, NEVER branded 'security', S-11)
repo.stack: Java 21 (the Signal messaging backend, a Dropwizard service) — the SIXTH instrumented stack and the FIRST Java datapoint (prior: Crystal/kemal, Python/pyccel, TypeScript/excalidraw, Go/caddy, Rust/ripgrep)
repo.size: Multi-module Maven (service / spam-filter / websocket-resources). The service module = 790 main .java files / ~71.4k main LOC (1,165 files / 112,459 code LOC incl. tests, scc). L-tier → COMPONENT-SCOPED.

analysis_scope: TWO scopes (mirrors the Rust/Go records' whole-graph-IF-8 + scoped-deep-families split): (1) IF-8 over the WHOLE service module — the real package-level import graph built from all 790 main .java files (46 graph nodes / 310 directed package-import edges; component = the immediate sub-package of org.whispersystems.textsecuregcm.*). (2) IF-6 / IF-10 / IF-7c / IF-3a over the cohesive 6-PACKAGE CORE = controllers (39 files / 9,000 LOC) + storage (102 / 15,535) + auth (32 / 2,361) + identity (4 / 319) + entities (98 / 3,914) + util (78 / 4,003) = 353 files / 35,132 LOC (the account/messaging request-path core). DynamoDB-backed → NOT an IF-2 target (db_status=n/a; live-DB IF-2 stays the discourse/Postgres environment-pending proof).
result_headline: Track-3 JAVA field sweep over signalapp/Signal-Server@adb5b6a4 (service module 790 files / ~71.4k LOC; 6-package core 353 files / 35,132 LOC): IF-8 FIRED on a genuine 33-package strongly-connected import cycle (a single 'big ball of mud' core containing all four Core packages controllers/storage/auth/identity; 38 distinct minimal 2-node mutual-import pairs close it; HIGH certainty, High severity) — recorded GATED / NOT filed (it would be ONE finding, not 33). The other four families stayed correctly SILENT: 0 genuine fires across IF-6/IF-10/IF-7c/IF-3a with 26 named structural suppressions total (IF-8 5 / IF-6 4 / IF-10 4 / IF-7c 4 / IF-3a 9) and 0 false defects. The decisive Java-specific comprehension: javac has NO compile-time package-cycle ban (vs Go's 'import cycle not allowed' + Rust/Cargo's 'cyclic package dependency'), so IF-8's positive fire here is REAL — the cleanest cross-language contrast in the corpus: cycles are structurally IMPOSSIBLE on Go/Rust, structurally PERMITTED-AND-PRESENT on Java.

fires.title: Giant cross-package import cycle: a single 33-package strongly-connected component within the service module
fires.severity_reason: the SCC contains Core packages controllers, storage, auth, identity (+ 29 others); per the shipped rule, any Core member ⇒ High.

representative_closing_edges.edge: controllers ↔ entities (LAYERING INVERSION — a DTO imports a controller)

fires.recorded_disposition: GATED / reported, not filed (field-sweep discipline + the moonshot gate ladder NOT run). It is a real, deterministic IF-8 fire — not a granularity artifact — because javac permits cross-package cycles within a Maven module. As a single dominant SCC it would be filed as ONE finding (the cyclic core), not 33; honest framing = 'a package-dependency big-ball-of-mud at the module level' — an architectural smell, not a runtime defect, and extremely common in mature Java. NOT a confirmed bug.

suppressed.reason: pure leaves (out-degree 0 into the namespace) — Tarjan placed each in its own size-1 SCC. A node with no participating back-edge cannot be in a cycle. Predicate-FALSE: IF-8's 'an acyclic chain (no back-edge) never fires'.

the <root> node has out-degree 33 / in-degree 0 — it imports the whole world but nothing imports IT back (it is the Dropwizard Application/Configuration boot class). A pure source can never lie on a cycle → its own size-1 SCC. This is exactly why the main loop's independent count (34 incl. <root>) collapses to the agent's 33 once the root pseudo-node is excluded — they agree.

these are imported-only external/generated namespaces (separate Maven artifacts / git submodules), not packages of this module — pure sinks with no edge back into textsecuregcm. Out of the cross-component cycle by construction.

suppressed.category: intra-package class cycles / self-loops (scoped OUT by the predicate)
suppressed.reason: edges where src_pkg == dst_pkg (a class in storage importing another class in storage) were dropped — intra-component recursion is NOT cross-component coupling. The fire is strictly cross-PACKAGE. Java permits intra-package class cycles freely; the predicate never inspects them.

suppressed.category: spurious phantom nodes from by-name class imports (graph-hygiene)
suppressed.reason: 3 by-name top-level class imports that resolved to a pseudo-node rather than a real subpackage; excluded from the SCC as non-package graph noise (they do not change the 33-member SCC or the 38 closing pairs).

per_family.java_specific_note: THE HEADLINE. javac has NO compile-time package-cycle ban — mutually-importing packages compile fine within one Maven module. So this 33-package SCC is a GENUINE structural fire, the OPPOSITE of caddy's suppressed 6-bucket Go component-SCC ( — a granularity artifact, since Go FORBIDS package cycles) and ripgrep's structurally-impossible clean Rust DAG ( — Cargo FORBIDS crate cycles). Maven/Gradle DO ban cross-MODULE cycles (the reactor build), and this sweep correctly scoped WITHIN the service module where package cycles are legal; the 3 modules (service/spam-filter/websocket-resources) are individually acyclic at the module level by build-tool enforcement, but that says nothing about intra-module package cycles — which is exactly where IF-8 lives for Java. 33 of the 35 real service subpackages form ONE giant SCC (only asn/gcp/jetty stay out); independent all-pairs mutual-reachability confirms 33/33 mutually reachable. The cycle is closed by a clear LAYERING INVERSION among others — entities/ConfirmUsernameHashRequest.java:15 (a request DTO) imports controllers.AccountController, and the domain model storage/Account.java imports up into auth + identity + entities while controllers import down into storage + entities. This is the textbook mature-Java big-ball-of-mud core. CROSS-VALIDATION: the main loop independently re-computed the package graph (a separate grep of every 'package'/'import' + a separate Tarjan SCC) and got the SAME 38 minimal 2-node mutual-import pairs and the SAME 33-member SCC (34 incl. the <root> source node) — two independent computations converged.

suppressed.reason: all three same-named 'Type' enums share ZERO members (pairwise symmetric difference == the entire union) — they are unrelated nested member-enums scoping distinct concepts (subscription-error kinds vs missing-MRM-data view vs subscription-lookup state) with no value-flow between packages. The idiomatic Java coincidental 'Type' homonym the predicate explicitly excludes (requires ≥1 shared member to even be a candidate, then behavioral entailment). NOT a divergent reimplementation of one shared allowed-value set.

suppressed.candidate: enum AvatarChange (canonical 'avatarchange') — twice, both in entities
suppressed.reason: membership genuinely DIVERGES (the AVATAR_CHANGE_ prefix differs → non-empty symmetric difference), so a naive cross-FILE name-keyed set-diff WOULD fire. But both live in the SAME package (entities) → no-redeclare-linter territory, not a cross-component behavioral divergence. The shipped predicate's same-component clause silences it (confirmed absent from controllers/storage/auth/identity/util).

suppressed.candidate: static-final Map UPDATE_EXPRESSION_ATTRIBUTE_NAMES — twice, both in storage
suppressed.reason: both Map constants live in storage (a v1/v2 schema pair). Same-component → suppressed; also a DynamoDB attribute-name aliasing Map, not an enumerable allowed-value SET, so not the IF-6 shape.

suppressed.candidate: fuzzy-stem family: ChallengeType / ErrorType / IdentityType / PushTokenType / VerificationType / CredentialType vs bare Type
suppressed.reason: a naive stemmer could lump all the *Type enums with bare Type; the predicate's canonical-name rule is EXACT (the OrderStatus/ORDER_STATUSES rule), so challengetype/errortype/identitytype/… are each their own bucket and none reaches ≥2 packages. No collision.

per_family.java_specific_note: FIRST Java datapoint for IF-6. Java has NO constraint on same-named enums across packages, and the idiomatic per-class nested enum named 'Type'/'Status'/'Result'/'State' makes coincidental homonyms ABUNDANT — 28 enum declarations across the 6 packages, but the ONLY cross-package canonical collision is the maximally-generic 'Type', which is exactly what the behavioral-entailment + zero-overlap-member clauses suppress. The dominant IF-6 form in Java is the enum (28 enums vs 9 static-final collections, 0 of which collide cross-package). The two genuine MEMBERSHIP divergences found (the 3 unrelated Type enums; the 2 AvatarChange enums) are suppressed for DIFFERENT named reasons — zero-overlap homonym vs same-component — i.e. both suppression arms fired on real Java code. A clean IF-6 here is a comprehension result (named mechanism per suppression), not a structural-impossibility artifact.

suppressed.predicate_false_clause: non-literal RHS / the IF-10 substrate does not exist
suppressed.reason: ZERO `static final boolean NAME = true|false` declarations exist anywhere in the 6-package core (or the wider module) — so there is no compile-time literal-boolean to fold into an if-test. The substrate is simply absent.

suppressed.category: 28 single-identifier `if(name)` whole-test conditionals — all RUNTIME booleans
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: non-literal RHS (method call / comparison / field read / param / constructor-assigned — not a compile-time constant)
suppressed.reason: 28 conditionals whose whole test is a single boolean identifier — every one is a local `final boolean` derived at runtime, a method param, or a constructor-assigned field, never a module-level literal `const`. Representative of the whole-module class.

suppressed.predicate_false_clause: bare literal with no const indirection (javac/IDE dead-code territory, not IF-10's const-fold gap)
suppressed.reason: zero `if (true)` / `if (false)` and zero `while (true)` in the 6-package core anyway.

suppressed.category: interface-implicit constants / mixed-condition operands / feature-flag-named constants
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: no substrate (no interface-body `boolean NAME=true/false`) + no mixed-condition const operand (test is never `CONST && x` / `CONST == y`)
suppressed.reason: hunted the third-form possibilities (an interface field `boolean X=true` used as a whole if-test; a const operand inside a mixed condition) — none present.

per_family.java_specific_note: The cleanest-possible structural negative for IF-10, and it is a LANGUAGE/IDIOM fact, not luck. IF-10's slice needs `static final boolean NAME = literal` used as the WHOLE if-test. Java has TWO idiom barriers that make this near-extinct in mature service code: (1) NO TRUTHINESS — a non-boolean scalar (`static final int/String/long`, of which there are 49 int + 264 String + 14 long here) can NEVER be a whole if-test; `if (INT_CONST)` does not compile, so the only literal-const if-test possible is a boolean one. (2) CONFIG IS INJECTED, NOT CONST — Signal-Server is a Dropwizard app; every real feature toggle is a runtime config value (WhisperServerConfiguration / DynamicConfiguration) read through a typed getter, not a `static final` literal. Result: zero `static final boolean` exists, so the substrate is absent. This MIRRORS the cross-language pattern for a DIFFERENT reason than the structural-impossibility families: where Go/Rust make IF-8 CYCLES structurally impossible (compiler ban), Java makes IF-10 CONST-FOLD-DEAD-BRANCHES idiomatically absent (no truthiness + injected config). javac genuinely does NOT const-fold-warn on `static final boolean F=false; if(F){}` (only some bare `if(false)`), so this WOULD be a real linter-silence gap IF the shape existed — it just doesn't here. The 28 `if(name)` near-candidates a surface tool would chase are ALL runtime booleans, each a named non-literal-RHS suppression.

suppressed.candidate: `public static void sleep(long i)` — `catch (final InterruptedException ignored) {}`
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: InterruptedException-idiom (precision adjudication, NOT a §23 letter-clause) — THE honest Java-specific near-candidate
suppressed.reason: the canonical Java idiom: a void sleep helper swallowing Thread.sleep's checked InterruptedException. It IS public + cross-package-consumed + an empty body, so a MECHANICAL empty-body+cross-consumed detector (the §23 letter) WOULD fire here. Precision-first SUPPRESSES: the swallowed exception is a cooperative thread-CANCELLATION signal, not an operation-result a caller consumes; `sleep` returns void and has no success/failure contract across the boundary — the polarity-opposite of a swallowed operation error. (Re-interrupting via Thread.currentThread().interrupt() is best practice; its absence is a code-smell, not a cross-boundary swallowed-operation-error.) See the IF-7c honest-note: IF-7c-on-Java needs an InterruptedException carve-out, the analog of Rust's `?`-propagation and Go's errcheck `_ =` carve-outs.

suppressed.candidate: `catch (final InterruptedException _) {}` around Thread.sleep in the worker-thread hot-loop pacing
suppressed.reason: same cancellation-signal idiom; the sleep paces the persister's node loop and the interrupt simply ends the pause. Not an operation-result swallow; suppress.

suppressed.candidate: `catch (InterruptedException e) {}` around this.wait() inside a re-checking while-loop
suppressed.reason: the wait() blocks until a valid configuration arrives on another thread; the surrounding `while (configuration.get() == null)` re-checks the condition every iteration, so a spurious interrupt just re-loops — the swallow changes no observable outcome. Cancellation-signal idiom; suppress.

suppressed.candidate: `catch (final NotPushRegisteredException _) {}` in a nested lambda inside a JAX-RS endpoint
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: cross-component-consumption moat FALSE (framework-dispatched entry point, not a cross-package import target) + best-effort-notification
suppressed.reason: the empty catch sits in a `.ifPresent(existingAccount -> { try { pushNotificationManager.send…(); } catch(NotPushRegisteredException){} })` lambda inside a @POST controller method. The enclosing exported method is a REST endpoint reached by the JAX-RS framework via HTTP routing — NOT imported/called by another package — so IF-7c's load-bearing cross-component-consumption moat is FALSE (the same elevation that distinguishes IF-7c from a no-empty linter). Semantically it is a deliberate best-effort verification-push (verification proceeds regardless). Suppress.

per_family.java_specific_note: FIRST Java datapoint for IF-7c, and it surfaced the stack's signature honest finding. Java's CHECKED-exception model makes `catch (InterruptedException) {}` the dominant 'empty catch' shape in service code (Thread.sleep / Object.wait / blocking-queue ops force the catch). 3 of the 4 empty/comment-only catches in the 6-package core are exactly this cancellation-signal idiom — and one of them, util/Util.java:254 `Util.sleep`, GENUINELY passes the §23 letter (public + cross-package-consumed + empty body), so a mechanical empty-body+cross-consumed detector WOULD over-fire on it. The precision-first adjudication suppresses it (a thread-interrupt is a cooperative cancellation signal, not a swallowed operation result a caller relies on) — the same shape as the prior stacks' language-faithful carve-outs (Rust's `?`-propagation behind `let _ =`, Go's CI-enforced errcheck `_ =`). The 4th near-candidate is a best-effort push inside a JAX-RS endpoint, suppressed on the cross-consumption moat (a framework entry point is not a cross-package import target). RECORDED LIMITATION → the honest unblock for IF-7c-on-Java: an InterruptedException-on-wait/sleep carve-out (a non-operation-result cancellation signal) before the §23 empty-body key, else the family over-fires on the most idiomatic empty catch in Java. 0 genuine fires / 4 named suppressions / 0 false defects.

suppressed.reason: de-facto-final string constant (never reassigned), used only within auth. Not mutable shared state → no 'change one, break the other' temporal coupling.
read by 3 packages but has ZERO writers → no temporal blast radius. An immutable shared constant is the polarity-opposite of mutable silent coupling.
`private` → unreachable outside its own class, let alone its package. Fails the ≥2-component arm.
private resource-name constant; single-class scope.
`public static final` Set — an immutable membership constant, not mutable shared state.

suppressed.candidate: Micrometer Counter/Timer static metric handles (~8 across the 6 packages)
suppressed.reason: static metric handles (e.g. CallRoutingControllerV2.CLOUDFLARE_TURN_ERROR_COUNTER, MessageController.INDIVIDUAL_MESSAGE_LATENCY_TIMER) are write-only telemetry registrations — no application read path, no 'change one break the other' data coupling.

suppressed.candidate: DynamoDB tables (167 table-name/tableName references across the storage layer)
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: reached through a typed interface (the explicit DynamoDB-via-Manager suppression)
suppressed.reason: EVERY direct DynamoDB write (putItem/updateItem/transactWrite/batchWrite/deleteItem) is confined to the storage package — verified by grep that controllers/auth/identity/entities/util issue ZERO direct table writes. All cross-package table access flows through constructor-injected typed *Store/*Manager classes (the explicit interface), so it is NOT silent coupling.

suppressed.candidate: Redis client / shared key namespace (auth/DisconnectionRequestManager, util/RedisClusterUtil, 14+ storage classes)
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: reached through a typed injected client
suppressed.reason: the Redis cluster is accessed through typed, constructor-injected client classes (the explicit interface), not by two packages poking a shared connection with no mediation.

suppressed.candidate: Application singletons (Managers/Stores) reachable cross-package (45 AccountsManager injections, 9 DynamicConfigurationManager, …)
suppressed.reason: every Manager/Store is a constructor-injected typed final field — the dependency-injection wiring IS the explicit interface the predicate requires, the opposite of an un-mediated global singleton.

per_family.java_specific_note: IF-3a is the most over-fire-prone family on the most permissive language, and it returned 0 fires — the strongest precision datapoint in this record. javac FREELY permits the mutable-static-field shape IF-3a hunts (`public static` non-final fields, mutable static singletons) — the exact shape Go's import-cycle ban and Rust/Cargo's dependency-cycle ban make structurally impossible cross-component. So unlike caddy (Go, which DID surface 1 LOW IF-3a global-globals fire) and ripgrep (Rust), an IF-3a fire here would be GENUINELY POSSIBLE, not a granularity artifact. The decisive finding: the language permits it, but Signal-Server's DISCIPLINED ARCHITECTURE forecloses it — (1) only FOUR non-final static fields exist across the entire 6-package / 35k-LOC core, NONE ever reassigned (all de-facto immutable, two private); (2) every DynamoDB write is confined to storage (verified: 0 direct writes from the other 5 packages), all cross-package access via constructor-injected typed *Store/*Manager classes; (3) every Manager/Store/Redis client is constructor-injected as a typed final field (Dropwizard DI = the explicit interface). A clean Shape-B reached by a DIFFERENT mechanism than the Go/Rust compile-time bans (discipline + DI vs compiler enforcement).

naive_vs_guarded_illustrative.guarded_fire_disposition: GATED (the IF-8 cyclic core — a real architectural smell, recorded not filed)
naive_vs_guarded_illustrative.note: A naive surface detector would over-fire on ~92 near-candidates across the five families: IF-8 ~34 (every high-fan-out hub + the 38 mutual pairs a naive 'any cross-import' flag would spam as 38 separate findings rather than ONE cyclic core), IF-6 ~2 (the divergent AvatarChange enums + the generic Type homonyms a bare name-keyed set-diff would fire on), IF-10 ~28 (every single-identifier `if(name)` a const-looking-token matcher would chase, all runtime booleans), IF-7c ~4 (every empty catch a no-empty linter flags — the InterruptedException idioms + the best-effort-push lambda; Util.sleep even passes the §23 letter), and IF-3a ~24 (every `public static` field + DynamoDB table + Redis client + injected singleton a 'shared global' pattern-matcher would flag). The guarded predicates resolve all of them to ONE honestly-disclosed structural fire (the cyclic core, gated) and ZERO false defects — each suppression names the load-bearing clause. The single fire is itself a PRECISION win: IF-8 correctly distinguishes a structurally-impossible Go/Rust cycle (suppressed/N/A) from a structurally-permitted-and-present Java cycle (fired).

key_java_findings: IF-8 / NO package-cycle ban → a REAL fire: javac permits cross-package cycles within a Maven module (the opposite of Go's 'import cycle not allowed' + Rust/Cargo's 'cyclic package dependency'). The service module's 35 packages form ONE 33-member strongly-connected component (a big-ball-of-mud core) — 38 minimal 2-node mutual-import pairs, all four Core packages (controllers/storage/auth/identity) inside it, closed in part by a layering inversion (a request DTO imports a controller, entities/ConfirmUsernameHashRequest.java:15). HIGH/High, recorded GATED. Maven/Gradle ban only cross-MODULE cycles (the reactor); intra-module package cycles — where IF-8 lives for Java — are unconstrained. INDEPENDENTLY cross-validated by a second Tarjan SCC in the main loop (same 38 pairs).

IF-10 / no truthiness + injected config → substrate absent: the only literal-const if-test Java allows is a boolean one (`if(INT_CONST)` does not compile; 49 int + 264 String + 14 long consts can never gate), and Dropwizard config is injected at runtime, not declared `static final` — so ZERO `static final boolean` constants exist to fold. javac does NOT const-fold-warn the shape, so it WOULD be a real linter-silence gap if it existed; it is idiomatically absent (the IF-10 analog of the Go/Rust IF-8 structural impossibility).

IF-7c / checked-InterruptedException is the dominant empty catch → needs a carve-out: Java's checked exceptions force `catch (InterruptedException) {}` on Thread.sleep/Object.wait, making it the signature 'empty catch'. util/Util.java:254 `Util.sleep` even passes the §23 letter (public + cross-consumed + empty), so a mechanical detector over-fires; precision-first suppresses it as a thread-CANCELLATION signal (not a swallowed operation result). HONEST unblock: an InterruptedException carve-out before the empty-body key — the analog of Rust's `?`-propagation and Go's errcheck `_ =` carve-outs.

IF-6 / coincidental nested 'Type'/'Status' homonyms: the idiomatic per-class nested enum makes same-named cross-package enums abundant (28 enums), but the only collision is the generic 'Type' (zero shared members → zero-overlap homonym, behavioral entailment absent). Both suppression arms fired on real code (zero-overlap homonym + same-component) → 0 fires.

IF-3a / DI + discipline forecloses what the language permits: Java freely allows mutable static singletons (the IF-3a shape Go/Rust ban structurally), but Signal-Server confines all mutable state to storage behind constructor-injected typed *Manager/*Store classes (Dropwizard DI = the explicit interface), with only 4 de-facto-immutable static fields in the whole 35k-LOC core → 0 fires. The most over-fire-prone family on the most permissive language stayed silent.
honest_assessment: This is the SIXTH instrumented stack and the FIRST Java datapoint, and it is the corpus's cleanest LANGUAGE-CONTRAST record: the same IF-8 detector that correctly STAYED SILENT on Go (caddy — a suppressed component-SCC artifact, since Go forbids package cycles) and Rust (ripgrep — a structurally-impossible clean DAG, since Cargo forbids crate cycles) here correctly FIRED on a genuine 33-package strongly-connected import cycle, because javac imposes no package-cycle ban — demonstrating comprehension of each language's structural rules rather than a fixed keyword pattern. The IF-8 fire is real (independently re-computed twice, 38 mutual-import pairs converged) but is an architectural smell, not a runtime defect, and is recorded GATED / not filed (the moonshot gate ladder is not run; AGPL copyleft raises the upstream bar further). The other four families stayed correctly SILENT with 26 named, mechanism-grounded suppressions — most tellingly IF-3a (the most over-fire-prone family, structurally POSSIBLE on Java, foreclosed only by Signal-Server's DI discipline) and IF-7c (where util/Util.java:254 `Util.sleep` passes the deterministic §23 letter yet is precision-suppressed as the checked-InterruptedException cancellation idiom — surfacing the honest IF-7c-on-Java unblock: an InterruptedException carve-out). Net: 1 genuine GATED fire (IF-8 cyclic core) + 0 false defects + 26 named suppressions across all five cross-module families — a high-precision, mechanism-grounded result that adds Java (a no-cycle-ban, checked-exception, DI-config stack) to the four prior stacks and sharpens the cross-language structural-comprehension story.

totals.genuine_fire_disposition: GATED (IF-8 cyclic core — reported, not filed)
totals.counts_note: Counts summed from the structured suppressed[] array lengths (authoritative). The single fire is the IF-8 strongly-connected component (ONE finding, not 33 packages × N edges). Naive-overfire ≈92 across families; guarded → 1 gated fire + 0 false defects.
